digests.weekSummary: A mostly quiet week dominated by AWS regional rollouts, infra version bumps, and enterprise/compliance features, plus a wave of OpenAI/Google blog and policy posts with no new developer capabilities. The few genuine openings were on the periphery: Twilio's new phone-reachability lookup API, Vercel making Sandbox data downloads free, Vercel's new Web Analytics API and open AI Gateway leaderboard data, Chat SDK's Slack adapter, and AgentMail's marketplace integration — each a concrete, buildable opening for a solo developer.

A quiet week dominated by routine AWS infrastructure rollouts, GitHub UI polish, and Anthropic admin/ops tweaks — almost none of it opens new capability for solo builders. The few genuine openings are Amazon Location Service's new address/drive-thru data, a strong new text-accurate image model (Seedream 5.0 Pro) now trivially callable via Vercel's AI Gateway, and the new Lovable-to-Vercel deploy integration creating a fresh, non-technical user segment worth building tooling around.
Nothing new published across any tracked source this week.
